understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to move a pushable object or controllable piece onto the goal tile(s) marked with a '+', or to achieve another explicit win condition as defined by the engine. In this level, the main activity was switching the selected piece between two positions, but the ultimate objective required either reaching a target position or meeting another level-specific condition. The game ended when the step budget was exhausted without achieving a clear WIN state.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was executed with directional actions (MOVE_LEFT, MOVE_RIGHT, etc.) and was constrained by grid-based walls or obstacles—attempting to move into a blocked tile resulted in no change. Pieces could be directly selected via the CLICK action, which teleported or switched focus between specific positions ([18, 21] and [36, 21] in this case). The step budget was finite, and each action decremented the remaining steps. Actions that did not result in movement (due to being blocked) still consumed steps. There was no evidence that pushing was possible in this level, as only selection and movement were observed.
